A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD for PLAINTIFF JOSEPH SOTO:
Counsel Horne's motion to withdraw as counsel of record is granted.

There is good cause to allow the withdrawal given the current conflict between Mr. Horne and Mr. Soto. Plaintiff Soto is not prejudiced since the case settled and Ms. Armendariz is still representing him.

The court will not order how the parties effectuate their settlement, i.e., whether Mr. Horne needs to sign the settlement check. The parties need to work that issue out.

Order is signed.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-26-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Philip S. Horne, esq. (415-874-9800) for Plaintiff.
Timothy B. Pickett, esq. (619-231-9800) of Harmeyer Law Group for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs P55 HOTEL OWNER LLC, PARC 55 LESSEE LLC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Negligence Per Se. "Under the doctrine of negligence per se, the plaintiff 'borrows' statutes to prove duty of care and standard of care." (David v. Hernandez (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 578, 584.) The FAC adequately pleads the negligence per se doctrine; the demurrer is overruled.

Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress. This count of the FAC does little more than recite the tort's elements from a jury instruction (id. at 14:6-20); the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
